case_number: Constitutional Petition is dismissed as not

chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI C.P. No.D-5354 of 2023 ___________________________________________________________________ Date Order with signature of Judge ___________________________________________________________________ FRESH CASE: 1. For order on CMA No.27085/2023 (Urgent). 2. For order on office objection No.27. 3. For order on CMA No.24613/2023 (Exemption). 4. For hearing of main case. ----------- Dated; 6th December 2023 Mr. Muhammad Sajjad Abbasi, Advocate for Petitioner. -*-*-*-*-*- 1. Urgency granted. 2. Learned counsel for the petitioner undertakes to comply with office objection before the next date of hearing. 3. Exemption granted subject to all just exceptions. 4&5. After arguing the matter at some length and while confronted as to maintainability of instant petition, particularly in view of the fact that the Ordinance upon which the reliance is being placed and the directions are being sought in respect of complaint made under Section 4 of the Protection of Parents Ordinance, 2021, which appears to have been lapsed, whereas, an FIR has already been registered by the petitioner against his sons, learned counsel for the petitioner does not press instant petition and submits that he will pursue his remedy available under the law. Accordingly, instant Constitutional Petition is dismissed as not pressed alongwith listed application. It is, however, expected that the authority where petitioner has instituted legal proceedings, including police, shall provide protection to the petitioner in accordance with law. ACTING CHIEF JUSTICE J U D G E *Farhan/PS*
